rationale: The revisions eliminate the vendor shield making employers liable for third-party tools. This control compels the organization to validate vendor AI systems for fairness and compliance ensuring the employer actively manages the risk rather than relying solely on vendor assurances.

Employers are responsible for the discrimination caused by the tools they procure. This control requires the collection of assurance inputs such as third-party model vetting and provenance attestations to identify risks in the software supply chain before deployment.

Employers are liable for AI-driven discrimination. This control establishes the fundamental tenet of adopting quantitative fairness metrics and enforcing mitigation plans when disparities exceed defined thresholds serving as the first line of defense against discriminatory outcomes.

Liability requires proof of diligence. This control mandates the evaluation of model performance and harms across demographic slices and domains providing the evidentiary record that the employer tested for discrimination prior to use.

Governance must extend to procurement to prevent liability. This control categorizes suppliers by risk and mandates due diligence assessments of their compliance posture ensuring that only vendors capable of meeting the employer's non-discrimination standards are onboarded.
